attacking_style: Pure sprinter who contests bunch finishes rather than initiating attacks. Wins from adjusted positioning in final 2km, learning from earlier positioning errors to come from back with higher speed.
team_tactics: Relies on teammates for peloton control to set up sprint finishes, but team protection collapses on moderate climbs (dropped at 40km despite team presence). Limited dedicated leadout evidence in observations.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages only. Exposed and dropped on even moderate terrain well before final climbs. Cannot survive in reduced groups when climbers accelerate, even when initially present.
vulnerability: Moderate climbing exposes him catastrophically (dropped 40km out, UAE Tour). Crash-prone in technical sprint finishes (barriers contact at 1.2km). Positioning desperation visible when outmatched in sprint quality.
key_patterns: Dropped on moderate terrain well before decisive climbs, losing contention entirely (UAE Tour 40km out)
Positioning errors and crashes in sprint finishes reduce win conversion rate (barriers crash, contested finish despite no win)
Legitimate WorldTour sprint threat with multiple 2018 wins against top-tier fields (4 WT wins documented)
Adaptive positioning strategy: learns from mistakes, switching to back-to-front sprint approach with higher speed
form_trajectory: negative: 2018 showed 4 WorldTour wins and sprint competitiveness, but 2019-2020 marked by crashes, positioning errors, and terrain vulnerability without wins recorded
